name: watermark
description: A repeating text watermark over content (Ant Design Watermark parity). Ant draws the mark to a <canvas> in JS and tiles it as a background image -- forbidden by the strict CSP. This CSP-clean equivalent renders the mark as REAL tiled DOM: a wrapper positions the children and an aria-hidden overlay tiles the text as a flex-wrap of rotated <span>s (count repeats), pointer-events:none (never intercepts clicks) and user-select:none (cannot be copied). No canvas, no data-URI, no script. Params: text, count, rotate, opacity, color, gap. The mark text is author-typed (HTML-escaped). Server-first PARTIAL, zero JS, CSP-clean.
docs: Copies the watermark partial to your JTE root at lievit/watermark.jte, reached as @template.lievit.watermark(text=..., content=@`...`). Wraps content with a repeating, rotated, low-opacity text overlay that is aria-hidden, non-interactive (pointer-events:none) and unselectable. Tune count (number of tiled repeats), rotate, opacity, color, gap. CSP-clean (no canvas, no script). The source is yours: edit freely.
